key_facts: Founded 2013 by Han Xu; parent of Uper Energy (优得运维)
Series C: July 2023, led by Cathay Capital; follow-on from Source Code Capital
ENGIE (French state utility) invested in company
Three subsidiaries: Liansheng Power (asset development), Uper Energy O&M, Aopu Storage (BESS)
Operations in SE Asia, Middle East, and Europe
China's largest third-party O&M service provider (by some measures)

funding_history.notes: Lead by Cathay Capital (France-China crossover fund); ENGIE (French utility) also invested — signals European O&M market expansion
Multiple rounds from inception to Series C

business_model_notes: Third-party O&M platform with ENGIE strategic backing for international expansion. Liansheng/Unisun is not tied to any single OEM (unlike Sungrow iSolarCloud or SolisCloud), enabling it to service multi-brand solar portfolios. Revenue: annual O&M service contracts per GW managed (industry benchmark: ~CNY 30,000–50,000/MW/year for C&I; ~CNY 15,000–25,000/MW/year for utility). 10 GW × CNY 30,000/MW = ~CNY 300M theoretical O&M revenue at lower bound. Aopu Storage subsidiary positions for BESS integration into managed assets.
competitive_position: Independent third-party O&M leader. Key differentiator: ENGIE partnership opens European market; brand-agnostic serving is attractive to developers who installed mixed-brand solar. Competes with: Jinko Power (600 GW aspirations, integrated installer+O&M), Guoneng Rixin (software-only SaaS), and OEM-captive platforms.
research_notes: High confidence on funding data (Cathay Capital press release, Source Code Capital portfolio page). ENGIE involvement confirmed via ENGIE investor relations. 10 GW and 3,000+ stations from company website and Tracxn database. Total $50.8M raised confirmed via Crunchbase. Founded 2013 by Han Xu confirmed via Unisun website About Us.
